---
title: "glasso"
generated: true
description: "Graphical-LASSO sparse precision (inverse covariance)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.connectivity`](../nitrix-stats-connectivity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:glasso -->
```python
glasso(S: Float[Array, 'p p'], lam: float | Float[Array, ''], *, n_outer: int = 100, n_inner: int = 50) -> Float[Array, 'p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:glasso -->
Graphical-LASSO sparse precision (inverse covariance).

Solves `argmin_Theta <S, Theta> - log det Theta + lam ||Theta||_{1,off}`
(off-diagonal-only L1 penalty -- the standard / nilearn convention) by
Friedman/Hastie/Tibshirani (2008) block-coordinate descent: an outer sweep
over columns, each an inner lasso on the working covariance `W`, with the
precision recovered from the converged per-column coefficients.  No
factorisation, no cuSOLVER; all loops rolled ($O(p^2)$ graph), so it is
differentiable through the fixed iteration budget and compiles flat in `p`
(fine for connectome `p = 100-400`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:glasso -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `S` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | `(p, p)` sample covariance (e.g. `stats.covariance.cov` or a shrinkage estimate). Off-diagonal-only penalty fixes `W_jj = S_jj`. |
| `lam` | `float | Float[Array, '']` | — | — | Non-negative L1 penalty. Larger `lam` -> sparser `Theta`. |
| `n_outer` | `int` | — | `100` | Outer column sweeps and inner lasso sweeps per column (fixed budget; the defaults reach KKT stationarity to machine precision on well-scaled connectome covariances and can be reduced for speed). |
| `n_inner` | `int` | — | `50` | Outer column sweeps and inner lasso sweeps per column (fixed budget; the defaults reach KKT stationarity to machine precision on well-scaled connectome covariances and can be reduced for speed). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `p` × `p`. `(p, p)` symmetric sparse precision; the inactive off-diagonals are exactly zero (the conditional-independence graph).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:glasso -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
